title: Factory Method
description: Encapsulate object creation; swap implementations without changing callers.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Factory Method is a Gang of Four design pattern that provides a code-level abstraction for object creation. It has no infrastructure requirements and does not involve any cloud services, SaaS subscriptions, or runtime resources. The pattern is purely a software design approach implemented in application code, therefore incurring zero monthly operational costs.
provenance.adoptionCost: Factory Method is one of the simpler GoF patterns with low adoption complexity. Implementation typically requires: (1) defining an abstract factory interface/base class (1-2 hours), (2) implementing concrete factory classes (2-4 hours), (3) refactoring existing instantiation code to use factories (2-4 hours), (4) writing unit tests (2-3 hours). Total effort: 7-13 hours. With AI coding assistants like GitHub Copilot or ChatGPT, developers can generate boilerplate factory code and tests rapidly, reducing manual effort by 40-50%. At a blended developer rate of $75/hour and assuming 8 hours of actual work with AI assistance, the adoption cost is approximately $600. However, for teams already familiar with design patterns, this drops to 3-4 hours (~$250). The pattern has minimal learning curve, excellent documentation, and is widely understood in the industry.
provenance.licenseCost: Factory Method is a design pattern from the public domain 'Gang of Four' book (Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software, 1994). It requires no proprietary tools, frameworks, or licenses. Implementation uses standard language features available in any modern programming language (Java, C#, Python, TypeScript, etc.). All necessary development tools (IDEs, compilers, testing frameworks) are available as open source or with free tiers sufficient for implementing this pattern. No commercial licenses are required.

provides.reasoning: Factory Method enables polymorphic object creation where subclasses determine which concrete class to instantiate, using inheritance and runtime polymorphism for object creation.
The pattern decouples client code from concrete class instantiation, allowing clients to work with interfaces/abstract classes rather than specific implementations.
Factory Method exemplifies the open-closed principle by being open for extension through new product types via subclassing while keeping existing client code closed for modification.

requires.reasoning: Factory Method fundamentally requires inheritance support as the pattern works by having subclasses override the factory method to return different product implementations.
The pattern requires polymorphism to allow the factory method to return different concrete types through a common interface, essential for letting subclasses decide which class to instantiate.

supports_nfr.reason: Factory Method organises code behavior by encapsulating object creation, not data content; it applies regardless of whether PII is present in the system.
Factory Method is a structural code pattern; GDPR compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of GDPR requirements.
Factory Method is a structural code pattern; CCPA compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of CCPA requirements.
Factory Method is a structural code pattern; HIPAA compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of HIPAA requirements.
Factory Method is a structural code pattern; SOX compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of SOX requirements.
Factory Method operates below the persistence layer, managing object instantiation; read-your-writes consistency is enforced at the data store, not by this pattern.
Factory Method does not implement persistence; strict durability guarantees are provided at the infrastructure layer and are orthogonal to this object-creation pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Factory Method is a pure software design pattern independent of cloud infrastructure.
Factory Method is a fundamental GoF pattern implementable in any object-oriented or procedural language.
Factory Method is a code-level pattern applicable across all platform types.

factory_type.description: Abstract: Subclasses decide which class to instantiate (classic GoF). Concrete: Single factory with conditional logic. Parameterized: Factory method takes parameter to determine product type.

product_hierarchy.description: Single: Factory creates variations of one product type. Family: Factory creates related product families (approaches Abstract Factory pattern).

registration_strategy.description: Static: Product types known at compile time. Dynamic: Products registered at runtime via registry. Plugin: External modules can register new product types for extensibility.

lazy_initialization.description: Whether products should be created on-demand (lazy) or eagerly initialized. Lazy reduces startup time but adds runtime overhead; eager fails fast on configuration errors.
